programmes.show_description: Sketch conceals a large portrait on biker Les, Alice spreads Becca's favourite food on her leg, and Jay immortalises Ashley's best mate Georgia as a pin-up girl.
Mike Wozniak issues a stark warning. Lee Mack has a makeover. And there's a storm brewing on Taskmaster Island...
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Kevin Bridges take on Jon Richardson and Victoria Coren Mitchell. Spencer Jones joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
David Seelinger, the chairman and CEO of New Jersey-based EmpireCLS, which provides chauffeured luxury transportation, works among his staff posing as a reality show contestant.
Robert Powells, the president of Rocket Fizz Soda Pop and Candy Shops, the largest fizzy drink and sweet shop franchise in America, goes incognito among his staff.
Competing to host the best party in Grimsby are straight-talking Mel and Patrick, seasoned ravers Vanessa and Steve and lovebirds Amber and Ryan, whose matching pink outfits raise eyebrows.
In West Yorkshire, there are terrible jokes, comedy teeth, plate licking and a conga, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.
...Selling: Kim and Pete find it hard to raise their boys in a split-level property. Can the brothers help the house reach new heights as the couple try to level up?
The One with the Cheap Dress: Scott spends less than £100 for a wedding dress and £1800 on life-size plastic animals.
The Remote Island One: Dave wants to whisk his water phobic bride-to-be Rosie off to a remote island wedding. But it's not just any island...
Sam Dushey, the CEO of Shoppers World, goes incognito among his own workforce, seeking reassurance that his stores are performing to his high standards as the business grows.
Tim Betts, the CEO of Muscle Maker Grill, goes undercover in the restaurant chain that he recently bought. Is every branch following the same high standards?
Jack's Waterfront: In Michigan, Gordon Ramsay visits a lakeside restaurant in a summer hotspot, where the food fails to match the fantastic views. Can Gordon turn the tide?
Hannah & Mason's: Gordon Ramsay's rescue mission this time is Hannah & Mason's, a French bistro in the quaint little town of Cranbury, New Jersey.
...and Selling: Brand new series: A dream kitchen is at the top of Charlie, Renee and their daughters' wish list. First, can the twins add some flavour to their current place?
Breaking Barbie: Fraser urges Barbie to be kinder to Cat. Ben and Sunny balance work and play. Captain Kerry steps in when chef Anthony struggles to keep up.
After an epic final of dunking, dinosaurs and dodgy directions, who will win Greg's head?
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Tom Allen take on Sean Lock and Rosie Jones. Adam Riches as Sean Bean joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Paul Foot take on Jon Richardson and Angela Barnes. John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Wedding: It's the big day: Mark is getting married to Sophie. Mark and his best man, Jeremy, set off in the car to the church, with a very hungover Super Hans in the back.
Burgling: Mark rediscovers single life after his disastrous wedding to Sophie, and he and Jeremy become victims of crime.
Alice gives feline fan Luke a pawsome tattoo. Sketch rids Gavin of a saucy slogan. Glen tackles Hannah's four-letter faux pas. Firefighter Simon has a hot design.
After an epic final of dunking, dinosaurs and dodgy directions, who will win Greg's head?
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Tom Allen take on Sean Lock and Rosie Jones. Adam Riches as Sean Bean joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Rick Forman, founder and CEO of New Jersey-based discount clothing superstore Forman Mills, dons a disguise to go undercover in his workforce, posing as a reality TV contestant.
Jessica Herrin, CEO of Stella & Dot, a direct sales company in the jewellery and accessories field with headquarters in San Francisco, goes undercover alongside her employees.
Carl and Brianha bring the French Riviera to Bognor Regis, with lasers and a smoke machine. Justin and Karen do fine dining. And is Mark and Lisa's curry too hot for their guests to handle?
High-flyers Bob and Louise, youngsters Will and Kim, and Iranian ex-pats Pasha and Haleh compete in Cheltenham, in a week of massive portions, a tale of heroism and Persian rice pudding.
...Selling: While Jonathan gets to work on Mike and Nicole's property, Drew hunts for a dog-friendly home. Let's hope he doesn't bark up the wrong tree.
The Harry Potter One: Harry Potter fan Sean needs all the help he can get planning a spooky witches and wizards-themed wedding in a forest for his bride-to-be Nicole.
The One with All the Tattoos: Self-confessed tattoo addict Adam wants an unconventional wedding at a London tattoo convention. Dani wants a day in the grounds of a castle.
Jeff Young, the co-owner of custom signs company YESCO, goes undercover in his own business. He meets staff who have suffered misfortune but remain dedicated to their jobs.
Scott Gerber, the CEO of hospitality business the Gerber Group, goes undercover in his own organisation, to ensure it is maintaining the reputation that made it such a success.
The Black Pearl: A struggling seafood restaurant in Manhattan, The Black Pearl, is hampered by debts, kitchen problems and three owners who all want to run things their way.
J Willy's Bar & Grille: Gordon's got his work cut out at J Willy's in Indiana - most of the food is from a can or the freezer, and they're a million dollars in debt.
...and Selling: Brand new series: Jonathan and Drew come up with a way to dazzle Dennis and Marie's current home - including an unorthodox way of getting rid of an armchair...
It's Always Sunny in Grenada: Conflict is rife as Barbie pushes boundaries, Cat struggles to find her place, and Jared's personal challenges take their toll.
Five celebrities enter Greg Davies' lair to begin a bitter battle for glory. Alex Horne invigilates while trying to avoid a barrage of fruit.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Kerry Godliman take on Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson. With Susie Dent, Rachel Riley and Dictionary Corner guest Mr Swallow, aka Nick Mohammed.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Harriet Kemsley compete against Jon Richardson and Joe Lycett. Mat Ewins joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Spin War: Mark and Sophie return to work after their non-existent honeymoon and Mark tries to deflect the inevitable bad PR attached to a jilter.
Jeremy Broke: Jeremy runs out of money and hits a downward spiral of hunger, homelessness and criminality, while Mark needs to find a girlfriend before his birthday party.
